title: Using OpenMP with cpp11
text: cpp11 differs Rcpp connects C++ R. C++ function marked [[cpp11::register]] attribute, cpp11::cpp_register(), counterpart Rcpp’s compileAttributes(), scans sources writes two generated files: src/cpp11.cpp, registers native routines, R/cpp11.R, holds R functions call . example computes Euclidean norm row matrix, dividing rows across OpenMP threads.
code: . ├── DESCRIPTION                         # Package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                           # Function and dependency registration ├── R                                   # R functions │   ├── cpp11.R                         # Autogenerated R bindings by cpp11 │   ├── cpp11openmp-package.R           # Package documentation │   └── parallel_row_norms.R            # Exported wrapper around the compiled routine ├── README.md ├── cpp11openmp.Rproj ├── man                                 # Package documentation │   ├── cpp11openmp-package.Rd │   └── parallel_row_norms.Rd └── src                                 # Compiled code     ├── Makevars                        # Enable OpenMP     ├── Makevars.win     ├── cpp11.cpp                       # Autogenerated registration by cpp11     └── parallel_norms.cpp              # The OpenMP-parallelized cpp11 routine

title: Using OpenMP with cpp11
text: routine lives src/parallel_norms.cpp. reads input matrix writes results plain std::vector inside parallel region, converts vector R vector cpp11::as_sexp() region finished. Keeping conversion outside threads means R API never touched inside parallel region, makes routine thread-safe. #ifdef _OPENMP guard lets routine compile without OpenMP, case loop runs serially.
code: #include <cpp11.hpp> #include <vector> #include <cmath>  #ifdef _OPENMP #include <omp.h> #endif  [[cpp11::register]] cpp11::doubles cpp_row_norms(cpp11::doubles_matrix<> x) {   int n = x.nrow();   int p = x.ncol();   std::vector<double> out(n);    #pragma omp parallel for schedule(static)   for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {     double ss = 0.0;     for (int j = 0; j < p; j++) {       double v = x(i, j);       ss += v * v;     }     out[i] = std::sqrt(ss);   }    return cpp11::as_sexp(out); }
